# tinyproctor.js

> Minimal, self-hosted, dependency-free browser integrity monitoring for online exams.
> One static JS file + one collector endpoint you implement. MIT license. v1.0.0.

This file is the implementation contract for AI agents integrating tinyproctor.js.

## Files

- Library (minified, ~16 KB raw / ~6 KB gzipped): https://tanzir71.github.io/tinyproctorjs/tinyproctor.min.js
- Library (readable source): https://tanzir71.github.io/tinyproctorjs/tinyproctor.js
- Human docs: https://tanzir71.github.io/tinyproctorjs/docs.html
- Repo: https://github.com/tanzir71/tinyproctorjs

## Integration steps

1. Download tinyproctor.min.js and serve it from the project's own static assets (same origin preferred; no CDN required).
2. Add this tag to the exam/quiz page template (server-render the real IDs):

```html
<script src="/js/tinyproctor.min.js" data-tinyproctor
  data-endpoint="/collect"
  data-exam-id="EXAM_ID_HERE"
  data-attempt-id="ATTEMPT_ID_HERE"
  data-candidate-label="PSEUDONYMOUS_USER_ID"></script>
```

Monitoring auto-starts on load. No init call needed unless `data-autoinit="false"`.

3. Implement the collector endpoint (POST, same origin or CORS-enabled).

## Collector contract

The client POSTs JSON batches (Content-Type: application/json; also sent via
navigator.sendBeacon on page close — accept both). Respond 2xx to acknowledge;
non-2xx responses are retried with exponential backoff (at-least-once delivery —
dedupe on sessionId + clientTime + eventType + violationKey if needed).

Request body:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "clientVersion": "1.0.0",
  "kind": "batch",
  "sessionId": "tp_...",
  "examId": "...",
  "attemptId": "...",
  "candidateLabel": "...",
  "sentAt": "ISO-8601",
  "userAgent": "...",
  "language": "en-US",
  "screen": { "w": 1920, "h": 1080 },
  "timezoneOffsetMin": -360,
  "events": [
    {
      "clientTime": "ISO-8601",
      "eventType": "heartbeat|visibility|focus|fullscreen|devtools|clipboard|contextmenu|print|idle|custom",
      "violationKey": "tab_hidden|window_blur|fullscreen_exit|devtools_key|devtools_resize|copy_attempt|cut_attempt|paste_attempt|context_menu|print_attempt|page_idle|custom or null",
      "eventData": {},
      "pageUrl": "...",
      "visibilityState": "...",
      "hasFocus": true,
      "isFullscreen": false,
      "violationCount": 0,
      "integrityScore": 100
    }
  ]
}
```

Store at minimum: attemptId, sessionId, clientTime, eventType, violationKey,
integrityScore, violationCount, eventData (JSON), received_at. Index by attemptId.

Validation recommendations: verify attemptId belongs to an active attempt,
rate-limit per session, cap body at ~1 MB, reject events for submitted/expired attempts.

CORS: only needed if collector origin differs from exam page origin. Then handle
OPTIONS preflight and send Access-Control-Allow-Origin + Allow-Headers: Content-Type.
sendBeacon sends no custom headers and skips preflight.

## Key config (data-* attributes on the script tag)

- data-endpoint (required for reporting) — collector URL
- data-exam-id, data-attempt-id (recommended), data-candidate-label
- data-silent (default true; false shows a live score badge)
- data-heartbeat-ms (default 30000)
- data-idle-ms (default 60000), data-idle (default true)
- data-clipboard / data-contextmenu / data-print / data-devtools (default true)
- data-block-clipboard / data-block-contextmenu (default false — set true to prevent, not just detect)
- data-weights — JSON, e.g. '{"tab_hidden":20,"paste_attempt":25}' (unlisted keys keep defaults)
- data-format="form" — sends payload=<urlencoded json> for legacy PHP backends
- data-autoinit="false" — then call TinyProctor.init({...}) yourself

## JS API (optional)

- TinyProctor.init(config) / TinyProctor.get() / TinyProctor.destroy()
- instance.getState() → { integrityScore, violationCount, violationCounts, recentViolations, ... }
- instance.markViolation(key, data) — custom violations
- instance.requestFullscreen(el) — call from a user gesture
- config.onViolation(key, data, state) and config.onEvent(payload) callbacks

## Scoring

score = clamp(100 − Σ(weight × count), 0, 100). Default weights: tab_hidden 10,
window_blur 5, fullscreen_exit 25, devtools_key 20, devtools_resize 15,
copy_attempt 10, cut_attempt 10, paste_attempt 15, context_menu 5,
print_attempt 10, page_idle 5.

Important: scores are triage signals for human review, not proof of cheating.
Never auto-fail a candidate from a client-side event alone.
